channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BRELA Advances Digital Systems to Boost Formal Business Sector
item.description: As Tanzania marches towards its Vision 2050 goals, digital reforms are becoming a major focus for government agencies, especially BRELA, which is seen as the main gateway for formal business operation...

item.title: Ondo State Confirms Eba Island as Its Territory, No Conflict with Ogun over Oil
item.description: Ondo State Governor Lucky Aiyedatiwa don clear ground say Eba Island na part of Ondo State, and sey dem no dey quarrel with Ogun State over the oil wey dey there. Aiyedatiwa talk say their discussions...

item.title: Karnataka Women’s Commission notes low reporting of workplace harassment committees in Dakshina Kannada
item.description: During her four-day tour of Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka State Commission for Women chairperson, Dr. Nagalakshmi Choudhary, highlighted that despite there being over 3,000 workplaces and institutions i...

item.title: Georgina Njenga Shares Worrying Video Hinting at Recurring Past Issues After Valentine's Day with Ex Baha
item.description: Popular Kenyan content creator and former Machachari actress Georgina Njenga has left many fans concerned after posting a video on social media with a cryptic and emotional message. This comes shortly...

item.title: Kim Chiu and Paulo Avelino Discuss Their Chemistry and Preparation for 'The Alibi'
item.description: In a recent interview, Kim Chiu and Paulo Avelino shared insights about their collaboration in the latest ABS-CBN series, 'The Alibi.' Both actors emphasized how their natural on-screen connection mad...

item.title: Heavy Rains Cause Flight Delays and Flooding in Nairobi
item.description: Overnight heavy rains in Nairobi have caused major disruptions at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, affecting several Kenya Airways flights. Some planes were diverted to Mombasa as a result of the ...

item.title: Starmer draws on Iraq experience in formulating UK approach to Iran
item.description: When Keir Starmer addressed the House of Commons regarding the situation in Iran, he emphasised the lessons learned from the Iraq War, signalling a cautious approach to military involvement. The compa...

item.title: Cross River Government Pauses Taskforce Operations Following Drivers’ Complaints
item.description: The Cross River State Government don don suspend all activities of taskforces wey dey monitor commercial transport and ticketing for the state. This decision come after commercial drivers for Calabar ...

item.title: Lagos Deputy Governor’s Office Allocates N4.2 Billion For Furniture And Microphones In 2026 Budget
item.description: For the 2026 fiscal year, Lagos State government don earmark N4.247 billion for the Deputy Governor’s office to buy office furniture and 30 microphones. This na addition to N97.5 million wey dem don a...

item.title: Government Prioritises Completion of Delayed Water Projects to Stabilise Supply
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile has emphasised that the South African government is prioritising the completion of delayed water infrastructure projects to improve the country's water supply and syste...

item.title: Debate over increasing the number of MPs in the Australian Parliament
item.description: The Albanese government is proposing to expand the Australian Parliament by adding 40 new MPs before the next federal election. While the Liberals have pledged to oppose the plan, The Nationals have i...

item.title: Adrian Nocum Earns First PBA All-Star Selection After Impressive Rise with Rain or Shine
item.description: Adrian Nocum, a 26-year-old guard for the Rain or Shine Elasto Painters, has earned his first PBA All-Star appearance, marking a significant milestone in his young professional career. Drafted 24th ov...

item.title: Oil Prices Soar Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure and Middle East Conflict
item.description: Oil prices are surging dramatically as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to tankers due to ongoing military actions in the Middle East. This vital waterway, through which roughly a fifth...

item.title: Perceptions of Housework Division Vary Between Husbands and Wives
item.description: Research into household labour often shows divergent results depending on who is asked. A recent study analysed 24 years of data from the US Panel Study of Income Dynamics, focusing on married, dual-e...

item.title: BBC Persian Issues Correction After Mistranslating 'Regime' as 'People' in Pete Hegseth's Iran Speech
item.description: The British Broadcasting Corporation's Persian service made a significant translation error during a live broadcast of U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth's recent speech on Iran. Hegseth referred to t...

item.title: Peter Obi Plans Legal Challenge Against Electoral Act 2026 Over Election Concerns
item.description: Former Anambra State Governor and ex-presidential candidate, Peter Obi, don yan say him dey plan take court action to challenge the Electoral Act 2026. Obi talk say some provisions for the law fit aff...

item.title: Casric Stars reach Nedbank Cup semifinals after defeating AmaZulu
item.description: Casric Stars have once again proven themselves as formidable opponents in the Nedbank Cup, extending their reputation for giant-killing. Following their shocking elimination of Orlando Pirates, the Kw...

item.title: Life EV Acquires Rad Power Bikes Following Bankruptcy Filing
item.description: Life Electric Vehicles Holdings (Life EV) has officially acquired Rad Power Bikes, including its intellectual property, inventory, and certain operating assets, for $13.2 million. Rad Power Bikes, a p...

item.title: PM Modi Criticises Trinamool Following President Murmu's Remarks on Political Conduct
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has strongly criticised West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee and the Trinamool Congress following remarks made by President Droupadi Murmu, calling certain actions '...

item.title: The Enduring Power of 'Women Without Men': Shahrnush Parsipur’s Feminist Novella in Context
item.description: Shahrnush Parsipur’s novella 'Women Without Men' (Zanan bedun-e Mardan) remains a seminal feminist work, defying decades of censorship in Iran. Published in 1989, it was promptly banned, and Parsipur ...
